summary: Iteration 15 backend smoke — FAZ 4 (gift fields + /coupons/available), FAZ 5 (ship/undeliver + undelivered status), FAZ 6 (customer risk + block, IP/UA capture) tested end-to-end. Also regression on notification provider masking + merge-preserve. 14/14 pytest tests pass.

minor.issue: Fast-path return when total_orders==0 in _compute_risk() omits 'return_rate_pct' key. Main path includes it. Causes response-shape inconsistency which can break UI bindings (e.g., {risk.return_rate_pct?.toFixed(1)}). Suggest adding return_rate_pct: 0.0 to the early return.
tracking_number validation has min_length=3 → returns 422 (Unprocessable Entity) for shorter tracking numbers instead of a friendlier 400. Acceptable, just noting for UI consistency.

coupon_code is stored upper-cased but there is no server-side validation that the code actually exists in /coupons; a bogus 'WELCOME10' is accepted. Consider validating at create time if coupon_code is set.

action_items: Add 'return_rate_pct': 0.0 to the zero-order fast-path in backend/routes/customer_risk.py::_compute_risk() for response-shape consistency.
(Optional) Validate coupon_code against /coupons collection at POST /api/orders time so invalid codes don't end up on the order document.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/orders.py create_order does a best-effort IP extraction but trusts X-Forwarded-For blindly. This is fine for block enforcement in admin context but could be spoofed by clients. Consider verifying it against a trusted reverse-proxy list or using request.client.host when X-Forwarded-For is absent — already partially done.
routes/customer_risk.py _compute_risk fast-path response schema differs from full path (missing return_rate_pct). Standardize both returns.

routes/coupons.py POST /coupons/available builds a time-window query with $and/$or that is correct, but `first_order_only` and per-user limits are still evaluated per-coupon in a Python loop — fine for small coupon counts (max 50) but will not scale if there are hundreds of active coupons; consider pre-filtering via MongoDB pipeline later.

routes/notifications.py save_providers merge-preserve works: empty string or any value containing '****' is treated as masked and original value is kept. Verified via 3-step test (real save → masked GET → re-save masked → GET masked with same head/tail). Solid implementation.
seed_data_creation: Two temporary coupons (TEST15A*/TEST15B*) created and auto-deleted via fixture teardown. Temporary orders TEST_FAZ4 / TEST_FAZ5 deleted via fixture teardown. One IP-block record created and removed. One email-block record created and then deactivated via DELETE.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iteration15_faz456.py exercises: /api/coupons/available, POST /api/orders gift+IP+UA capture, PUT /api/orders/{id}/status?status=undelivered, POST /ship (valid+invalid cargo+404), POST /undeliver, /customer-risk (users/by-email/bulk/block/blocked/DELETE), block-by-IP rejects POST /orders with 403, and notifications provider masking+merge-preserve. Reuses fixtures for auth, coupon seeding, and order creation. All pass. notification_logs assertion is best-effort (logs may be trimmed).
rca of the issue: Not applicable — feature tests pass. Two minor consistency issues flagged for E1 to address at its discretion.
